summary: cursed-modules exfiltrates installer env, /proc/1/environ, and credentials to attacker C2 on install
details: The package executes attacker code on `npm install` via three lifecycle hooks (preinstall/install/postinstall all pointing at install.js) and again at require() through index.js. Both files run the same routine: shell out via execSync('find / -maxdepth 8...' and 'grep -rhoE...') to scan the filesystem, read /proc/1/environ, capture process.env and os.hostname(), and POST the collected data to a hardcoded webhook.site bucket (webhook.site/a9d21bb7-9e09-446c-a85d-85f071ebcb82). The tarball additionally ships recon.js, which harvests id/whoami, full process.env, ps aux, netstat, /etc/crontab, /root/.npmrc, /home/user/.npmrc, Verdaccio htpasswd/config, /proc/1/environ, and flag files and PUTs them to http://154.57.164.76:30728/api/modules/ECT-654321. rce.sh implements the operator side of a command-and-control channel against http://154.57.164.70:32447/api/modules/ECT-{654321,987654}, putting commands and base64-fetching results. publish-and-arm.sh automates `npm publish` of a follow-on version and registers a manifest labeled 'dependency-confusion'/'verdaccio-proxy' with the same C2, indicating an active dependency-confusion campaign against an internal Verdaccio registry. Installing this package on default settings exfiltrates installer secrets and grants the operator remote shell control.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
